summary: Patrick McKenzie outlines his planned blog schedule for the upcoming week, listing specific topics for each day. He explains the scheduling is partly to avoid a Sunday crunch where church, family, bug fixes, writing, volleyball, and Mario Galaxy all compete for his time. The post gives a window into the juggling act of running a micro-ISV alongside a full-time life in Japan.
key_points: McKenzie experiments with pre-scheduling blog content to manage his packed weekly commitments
His Sunday priorities reveal the hierarchy: God, family, Mario Galaxy, customers, health, and professional duty all rank above timely blogging
The planned topics span Google Optimizer, SQL optimization for Rails, vendor recommendations, and product updates — showing the breadth of a solo founder's concerns
money_quotes: Something had to go and God, family, Galaxy, customer satisfaction, health, and professional duty won out over timely blog updating.
key_insight: A solo software founder's week is a constant triage between product work, content marketing, personal life, and everything else — and being transparent about that triage is itself good content.
